mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the industrial production index signal expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives higher corporate operating leverage and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are negative in more than 50% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, elevated labor market slack suppresses wage growth and household consumption expenditures, which compresses top-line revenue forecasts for consumer-facing firms and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months when UNRATE is above 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal accelerating labor market absorption; this raises aggregate disposable income and boosts aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month while UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observed instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound interest rate environment, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal a recovery in interest-sensitive residential investment; this early-cycle demand impulse lifts construction and durable goods sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt inflationary impulse that erodes real corporate profit margins and raises expectations of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following CPIAUCSL increases greater than 0.5% are positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real chained GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion and positive output gaps, which lifts corporate sales volumes and profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly changes in GDPC1 are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points within a three-month window signals an abrupt shift to monetary tightening; this raises corporate discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points within a 3-month window
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following such FEDFUNDS increases are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 200 thousand persons signals sudden labor market contraction and falling aggregate wage income; this reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 200 thousand persons month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following PAYEMS declines greater than 200k are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.
